fun_fact: India introduced new Solid Waste Management rules in April 2026 and they mandate that industries use waste as fuel — but this creates a perverse incentive to keep waste mixed rather than segregate it. Up to 40% of material burned in waste-to-energy (WTE) plants becomes toxic ash requiring special disposal, people living near WTE have adverse health impacts and this also affects the 1.5–4 mn informal waste workers whose livelihoods depend on segregating and not burning.
